properties.remark: ASOS STATION KOLF WOLF POINT AIRPORT.
ESTIMATED ONE INCH OF RAIN IN 30 MINUTES.
SPOTTER REPORTED HAIL STONES UP TO SIZE OF GOLD DOLLAR COIN, JUST LARGER THAN A QUARTER.
6TH ST. UNDERPASS FLOODED IN GLASGOW WITH VEHICLE STUCK IN THE WATER.
HEAVY DOWNPOURS ALONG WITH HAIL UP TO THE SIZE OF QUARTERS.

A survey team was sent out for a visually confirmed landspout. We received public and law enforcement reports at approximately 7:56 pm MDT, lasting about 10 minutes as estimated from radar imagery. No visible damage to properties or fields.
Location estimated by pictures sent in via Facebook. Flash flooding has been observed and is ongoing.
Mesonet station KIGM8 King Coulee.
Reports heavy rainfall and water running down numerous streets and area roads.
Reported 2.3 inches of rainfall, fields flooding.
Water reported over gravel roads in several locations west of Hinsdale.

BNSF Railroad Track Inspector reported multiple locations of track subgrade material completely washed out along a half-mile stretch of railroad at Hinsdale. Material was washed over US Hwy 2 and into Hinsdale. Train traffic has been suspended until repairs can be made. Time of impacts are estimated.
Trained Spotter in the town of Savage estimated wind gusts of at least 60 mph.
Also reported heavy rain, 35 mph wind gusts estimated.
Hail up to the size of quarters.
Hail up to the size of half dollars lasted for 20 minutes (5:30 pm to 5:50 pm).

Corrects previous tornado report from 2 NE Hinsdale (for exact time and location). Multiple trained spotters confirmed multiple funnel clouds, one of which was observed touching the ground and lofting a dirt cloud at its base. Tornado lasted for less than 1 minute.
Multiple trained spotters confirmed multiple funnel clouds, one of which was observed touching the ground and lofting a dirt cloud at its base. Tornado lasted for less than 1 minute.
Law Enforcement reported water over the roadway on US Highway 87 at Mile Marker 22.
Also reported 30-40mph gusts.
Picture of hailstone submitted via Facebook.
Also noted that hail was accumulating in town (at least one inch deep).
Facebook photos showed hail measuring up to ping pong ball size and accumulating on the roadways.
Via Facebook.
Ping pong ball size estimated with photo.
Spotter reported heavy locally heavy rainfall with 1 inch hail stones bouncing off the ground.
Reported at the Pine Hills RAWS site.
Spotter reported a hail stone of 3 inches embedded within a field of 1 inch sized hail.
Recorded at the Jordan Airport ASOS.
An old building wall structure fell during the passage of an outflow boundary and the associated thunderstorm.

storm lasted 15 minutes, 1 rain fell.

Davis weather station recorded a 63 mph wind gust. Spotter also reported dime size hail.
House siding blown off. Tree wind break blown away.
Facebook report.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 1 W Wolf Point. Hardcover of pickup bed lifted into back window causing damage, estimated gust 90 mph.
Hardcover of pickup bed lifted into back window causing damage, estimated gust 80 mph.
Power poles and trees reported to be knocked down, cross walk light near Northside Elementary School has been blown over. Facebook report. Time estimated from radar.
Power poles, trees reported to be knocked down. A crosswalk light near the Northside Elementary School has been blown over. Facebook report with time estimated from radar.
Facebook Report.
Corrects previous tstm wnd gst report from 3 E Wolf Point. This was the latest observation before instrument failed.
6 to 10 inch Russian Olive tree snapped at the base of the trunk.
Several large trees (equal to or greater than 2 ft diameter) either uprooted or snapped at the base of trunks at Airport Golf Club in Wolf Point.
Also, neighbors horse trailer was blown over.
Estimated wind gusts of 50 to 60 mph.
1.85-2 rain, quarter to ping pong size hail, wind speeds of 60+ mph.
1.85-2 inches of rain, quarter to ping pong size hail, wind speeds of 60+ mph.
ASOS station KGGW Glasgow Airport (ASOS).
Some of the damage that the spotter observed: A lid on a grain bin was blown away. Some trees down. At a nearby school, the flat top roof was lifted off the building, but not completely detached.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 5 S Opheim. Time estimated from radar. Trees were stripped.

0.44 Rainfall and wind gust to 45 mph.
ASOS reported wind gust of 70 mph between 6:48 PM and 7:48 PM, associated with thunderstorms.
Thunderstorm outflow wind gust of 58 mph reported by Mesonet.
Public reported vis social media. Wind speed estimated at 70 mph. Open faced barn/shed blown down. Shed was sitting over their tractor, four wheeler, and bike.
Public reported, via the Wibaux DES coordinator, a barn that was knocked over near Hodges. Time estimated by radar. Winds estimated at 60 mph.
